title: Module 1
content:
Fundamentals of Vehicles
Engine basic concepts (4 strokes),
Starting & charging system,
Electronic dashboard instruments, on board diagnostic system,
Open loop and closed loop systems components for electronic engine
management,
Vehicle motion control, Digital cruise control, current trends in modern
automobiles.

title: Module 2
content:
Engine management systems
Basic sensor arrangement, types of sensors such as oxygen sensors, crank
angle position sensors, Fuel metering/ vehicle speed sensors, flow sensor,
temperature, air mass flow sensors, throttle position sensor, solenoids etc.,
algorithms for engine control including open loop and closed loop control
system, electronic ignition, EGR for exhaust emission control.

title: Module 3
content:
Automobile Chassis Electronic Control System
Principle of electronic braking, automatic transmission electronic control
circuit
cruise control circuit, the electronic steering control theory
ABS, ASR, ESP, and other electronic control method
Auto Body Electronic Control Technology: Automotive central locking and
anti-theft system control technology. Air bag technology

title: Module 4
content:
Latest  Automotive Instrumentation
Concepts of hybrid technology
Introduction to ADAS (Automatic Driver Assist Systems)
Introduction to electric vehicles, classifications
Modern Automotive Instrumentation   Input and Output Signal
Conversion Multiplexing Multi rate Sampling Advantages of Computer-
Based Instrumentation Display Devices (any one). Fuel Quantity
Measurement,
Coolant
Temperature
Measurement
Oil
Pressure
Measurement Vehicle Speed Measurement   High-Speed Digital
Communications (CAN) CAN Network Trip Information Computer
Telematics  GPS Navigation  The GPS System Structure.
